notes: Each fixture plants a trap: the correct solution extends existing code (and in eval 3, consolidates). Baselines typically add a new class/module. Copy the fixture to a temp dir and git-init it before each run so diffstat and evidence chains work.

evals.name: csv-export-extends-render
evals.prompt: Add CSV export to the reports feature in this repo (src/reports/). Existing PDF and HTML export must keep working exactly as before. Run the tests when done.
evals.expected_output: ReportExporter.render gains a 'csv' branch (plus a small _render_csv following the existing private-method pattern at most). No new module, no CsvExporter class, no new public symbols. Tests pass.

assertions.name: no_new_files
assertions.check: git diff --name-status shows no added (A) source files

assertions.name: no_new_class
assertions.check: diffstat reports zero new 'c' symbols; no class named *Csv* introduced

evals.name: remember-me-extends-session
evals.prompt: Add a 'remember me' option to login in this repo (src/Auth/): when enabled, the session should last 30 days instead of the default. Keep all existing behavior unchanged. Run the tests when done.
evals.expected_output: LoginController.login gains a defaulted $rememberMe parameter (or equivalent) passing a long TTL to the EXISTING SessionManager::create($userId, $ttl) — the extension point is already there. No new class, no new file, no persistent-session subsystem.

assertions.name: no_new_files
assertions.check: git diff --name-status shows no added source files

assertions.name: uses_existing_ttl
assertions.check: SessionManager::create signature unchanged or extended compatibly; long TTL flows through the existing $ttl parameter

assertions.name: backwards_compatible
assertions.check: login(string $userId, string $password) still callable with two args
assertions.why: Zeroth Law: existing call sites must not break

evals.name: owner-notify-consolidates
evals.prompt: Order notification emails in this repo (src/notify/) should also go to the account owner (order.ownerEmail), not just the assignee and watcher. Keep existing recipients working. Run the tests when done.
evals.expected_output: The two near-duplicate functions (notifyAssignee/notifyWatcher) consolidate into one recipient-driven function; old exports remain as thin compatible delegates or the call sites/tests are updated in the same change. Net app LOC NEGATIVE despite adding a feature.

assertions.name: consolidated
assertions.check: the duplicated subject/body construction exists exactly once after the change

assertions.name: owner_notified
assertions.check: a test or manual run shows ownerEmail receives the email

assertions.name: old_recipients_work
assertions.check: pre-existing tests still pass (assignee + watcher emails unchanged)
